programme.title: Amor perdido
programme.desc: A beautiful young girl named Amalia.(played by actress Amalia Aguilar). with a talent for Rumba, wants to become a glamorous cabaret singer. Her dream is encouraged by her best friend, Ernesto, (Tito Novaro), who also happens to be a gifted composer. While working towards her career goal, Amalia meets the ultimate "bad-boy", a dashing gangster named Luis, (Victor Junco). She is quickly swept off her feet and falls in love with him. Unfortunately, Luis has a jealous streak and a rather possessive personality, especially when it comes to women. He also has a prior girlfriend named Celia, (Yadira Jimenez). Celia also happens to share similar traits with Luis and is filled with jealous rage toward Amelia. So much so that she causes her rival in love to have an accident which leaves Amalia's face horribly scarred-so much so that she must now wear a mask in public. Filled with shame and fear of rejection, she decides to leave Luis without explanation or saying farewell, Her actions anger her former lover, who thinks she left him for better career opportunities. So, he decides to kill poor disfigured Amalia.

programme.title: Capítulo 131
programme.desc: Mia reflects on her and Miguel's precious moments. Diego decides to leave the band and Roberta is blamed for it. Alma decides to be the dance group's choreographer. Gastón calls Mia out of class, she cries about Miguel and he comforts her.

programme.title: Capítulo 132
programme.desc: Mia start a campaign/fundraiser to help save the Chapultepec Forest. Teo tells the Lodge that José was the one responsible for the anonymous letters out of fear and spite. Lupita decides to stay in school. Gastón gives Mia a present. Miguel becomes suspicious of Gastón.

programme.title: Capítulo 133
programme.desc: Diego tells his mom of his love for Paola; she asks to meet her. Gastón overhears José and Vico talking about him; he threatens José. Miguel is allowed access to Franco's files. Roberta tells Facundo not to let Diego into his band. León gets pictures of Paola with Diego.

programme.title: Capítulo 134
programme.desc: León threatens Paola on the phone and demands to speak to Diego. Mia distracts Gastón so the band won't be seen; he embraces her. Roberta tricks the principal to stop her mother from teaching choreography at the school. Mia gets mad at Miguel for telling the everyone that her and Gastón are dating.

programme.title: Capítulo 135
programme.desc: León give Paola a few days to leave Diego. Teo is told by the Lodge to steal a teacher's wallet; José is later blamed. Miguel tells Gastón to stay away from Mia and cleverly threatens him. Gastón and Renata conspire against Enrique.

programme.title: Capítulo 136
programme.desc: Roberta helps Diego ditch school. Alma brings Mia from school to be the new main fashion model. José is threatened with expulsion if she doesn't admit to theft. Diego and Paola plan to run away together. Renata confronts Enrique about his past.

programme.title: Capítulo 137
programme.desc: Roberta asks Paola to tell Diego the truth of her relationship with León. Diego leaves with Paola. Franco tries to pay Alma to stay away from Mia. Alma and Roberta cry for their loves. Gastón tells Mia he has a love he can't get out of his head; he embraces her a second time.

programme.title: Capítulo 138
programme.desc: Alma reveals to Franco, in a big way, how she spent his bribe money. Teo steals all the other teachers wallets and puts them in Pascual's desk in order to save José. Roberta gets Mabel to go look for Diego. Galia, Pascual and Pilar's wife and mother respectively, becomes the new English teacher.

programme.title: Capítulo 139
programme.desc: The Lodge is upset that they failed in getting José expelled. José meets her tutor face to face. Franco finds out that Mia like a man; not a boy at the school. Mia, in anger, tells her father she's in love with Gastón; however Gastón is not worried. Later, when Mia becomes upset at the sight of Viviana with Miguel, Gastón kisses her.

programme.title: Capítulo 140
programme.desc: Gastón kisses Mia for the first time. Franco believes that Alma is cheating on Carlo. Nico tells Teo he might like José more than he knows. Mia tells Gastón she's harassed by Miguel at school. Franco follows the alleged cheater and finds out he's in charge of Mia's model campaign. Raquel and Giovanni begin to date; Tomás and him fight. Teo is saved by a fellow Lodge member. Gastón tells Mia he wants to take care of her for life.

programme.title: La cocina económica
programme.desc: Jorjáis deja su sillón en la entrada del edificio de los vecinos, pero en un momento de descuido, desaparece. Lorena abre una cocina económica.

programme.title: Una pareja real
programme.desc: Los Rivers abren la correspondencia de los López Pérez y se enteran de que les embargarán la lavadora. ¿Magdalena se atreverá a demandarlos?

programme.title: La bocinota
programme.desc: Una refaccionaria se instala en la accesoria del edificio. La inauguración causa molestia entre los vecinos. Rocko y Arturo aprovechan para conseguir chamba en el local.

programme.title: Bienvenido doctor
programme.desc: El doctor Rafael Rivas se muda al edificio y Magdalena no deja pasar la oportunidad para conseguir una consulta gratis abusando de la confianza del nuevo inquilino.

programme.title: Una de terror
programme.desc: Silvita trata de mantener limpios los pasillos del edificio, ya que Lorena y Magdalena sacaron todas sus macetas. Mientras Luis trabaja, Pedro y Cris verán una película.

programme.title: Bibi maneja
programme.desc: Bibi quiere aprender a conducir, lo que a los P. Luche les parece una excelente idea, solo para que Bibi se convierta en su chofer. Sin embargo, cuando llevan esto al extremo, Bibi sufre un pequeño accidente.

programme.title: El funeral del Merengues
programme.desc: En el velorio de Merengueti, un empleado de Industrias Camerino, Ludovico se da cuenta de que el cadáver tiene el reloj que perdió en la mañana e intenta quitárselo.

programme.title: Los candidatos
programme.desc: Silvia y Arturo compiten para ser el presidente del vecindario. Alejandra y Rocko se preocupan porque creen que han perdido su juventud y personalidad.

programme.title: Hoy juega México
programme.desc: La tía Oli visita a su sobrina Silvita y no se irá hasta conseguirle pareja. Germán organiza una reunión para ver fútbol, pero todos escapan cuando les pide cooperación. Silvita intenta imitar a su tía, mientras en su casa se arma un caos.

programme.title: Reunión de ex alumnos
programme.desc: Silvia organiza una reunión en su departamento para sus excompañeros, por lo que le pide a Pedro que se haga pasar por su novio para que no piensen que sigue soltera.

programme.title: Jorjáis roomie
programme.desc: Magdalena sospecha de que Lorena tiene un amante y convence a Silvia de seguirla para descubrir la verdad. Pedro le renta a Jorjáis la habitación de Luis para que tenga donde dormir, sin imaginar las consecuencias.

programme.title: La telenovela
programme.desc: Los vecinos rentan el edificio para la grabación de una telenovela y por accidente, Jorjáis resulta ser un gran actor compartiendo escenas con Renata Notni.

programme.title: Mi vieja
programme.desc: Luis le pide a Pedro que lo releve con un asunto. Pedro se confunde y actúa de manera exagerada. Germán evitará que dejen basura afuera del edificio.

programme.title: Nuevo inquilino
programme.desc: Un nuevo vecino llega al edificio para rentar uno de los departamentos y Silvia se enamora de él. Germán quiere tatuarse y pide recomendaciones.

programme.title: La venganza de la maestra
programme.desc: Alejandra descubre que la maestra de Morris es Belén, la misma maestra que ella molestaba. Chelo, la empleada doméstica de Silvia, tiene más suerte en el amor que ella.

programme.title: Arturo amuleto
programme.desc: Jorjáis cree que Arturo le trae buena suerte a su equipo de fútbol cuando va a los partidos, por lo que buscará la manera para que él esté presente en la final del torneo y así poder ganar.

programme.title: La bomba de agua
programme.desc: Los vecinos le piden a Germán que repare la bomba de agua del edificio para evitar pagarle a un plomero profesional. Jovita intenta que Pedro cambie sus hábitos alimenticios, pero él se rehúsa y hace una huelga de hambre.

programme.title: Echando cohetes
programme.desc: Los vecinos se molestan por el ruido que provocan los cohetes de una fiesta. Rocko se queja de que Alejandra no es cariñosa con él.

programme.title: El desalojo
programme.desc: Frankie se entera de que un famoso director está interesado en uno de sus guiones. Los López Pérez reciben una orden de desalojo. Germán decide irse a Estados Unidos tras una decepción amorosa.

programme.title: El regreso de Luis
programme.desc: Luis le pide a Silvia que sea su novia, Pedro lo convence de ser mejor persona y así conseguir su amor. Los López Pérez regresan a vivir al edificio y Germán les tiene a todos una sorpresa.

programme.title: Primera cita
programme.desc: Magdalena es la nueva jefa de seguridad y, debido a los robos en el edificio, instala cámaras con la ayuda de Germán. Silvita y Luisito tienen su primera cita.

programme.title: El cumple de Silvia
programme.desc: Silvia cumple años y aconsejan a Luis que no la felicite para llamar su atención. Los López Pérez no encuentran a Morris por ningún lado y sospechan que se ha ido de la casa.

programme.title: Luis pobretón
programme.desc: Luis se ha quedado sin trabajo, por lo que buscará una nueva actividad para invitar a Silvia a salir. Por otro lado, Luismi ha llegado solo al edificio.

programme.title: La novia de Pedro
programme.desc: Pedro invita a salir a Cris, la mejor amiga de Silvia, y organiza una cita doble con Luis y Silvita. La alcaldía instala una cámara de fotomultas y los flashazos apuntan directo al edificio.

programme.title: En una relación
programme.desc: Después de un tiempo de salir juntos, Luis le pregunta a Silvia qué son y quiere que se decida de una vez. La alcaldía instala lámparas de led en el alumbrado público y la no deja dormir a los vecinos.

programme.title: Beso fallido
programme.desc: Luis invita a Silvia a ver una película a su casa, pero Pedro interrumpirá su romántico momento, pues quiere recuperar su tele. Por otra parte, Arturo jugará póker con Germán, Jorjais y Benito.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: MeatEater - Bahamas Spearfishing
programme.desc: Hunter, author, cook and conservationist Steven Rinella treks into the world's most remote and beautiful regions, bringing game meat from field to table. Steve heads down to the Bahamas to spearfish with friends Kimi Werner and Cameron Kirkconnell. They explore the clear waters of the North Atlantic to bring home a wide array of fish species, including Mahi Mahi and grouper.

programme.title: Louisiana Spearfishing and Bowfishing
programme.desc: Steven Rinella returns to the coast of Louisiana to spearfish and bowfish with Ronnie Collins and Greg Fonts.

programme.title: MeatEater - Montana Muzzleloader Hunting for Mule Deer
programme.desc: Steven Rinella takes on Montanas week-long big game heritage season with a traditional muzzleloader.

programme.title: MeatEater - Texas Turkey with Luke Combs
programme.desc: Steven Rinella returns to the Yturria Ranch in south Texas, this time, to hunt turkeys and pigs with country.

programme.title: MeatEater - Nevada Mule Deer with Joe Rogan
programme.desc: Steven Rinella teams up with Joe Rogan in Nevada's high desert to bowhunt for elusive mule deer bucks. Surrounded by vast public lands and rugged terrain, they navigate the challenges of close-range stalking with their bows. With Joe's enthusiasm for archery and Steve's experience, the duo pushes their limits in pursuit of these wary deer, embracing the highs and lows of hunting in one of America's most unforgiving landscapes.

programme.title: MeatEater - Alaska Moose Part 1
programme.desc: Steve and Janis are deep in the wilderness of eastern Alaska on a challenging moose and caribou hunt. Joined by Janis' father, Papa Jani, who wants one last adventure before age catches up, they endure the highs and lows of remote hunting. With patience wearing thin and the weather turning, the group pushes through thick willows and foggy mornings, hoping for a bit of luck and a successful hunt before their journey's end.

programme.title: MeatEater - Alaska Moose Part 2
programme.desc: Steve and Janis are deep in the wilderness of eastern Alaska on a challenging moose and caribou hunt. Joined by Janis' father, Papa Jani, who wants one last adventure before age catches up, they endure the highs and lows of remote hunting. With patience wearing thin and the weather turning, the group pushes through thick willows and foggy mornings, hoping for a bit of luck and a successful hunt before their journey's end.

programme.title: MeatEater - Prince Of Wales: Fishing and Deer
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads back to his fish shack on Prince of Wales Island in Southeast Alaska, this time with friend and colleague Morgan Fallon. Known for his cinematography, Mo now wants to switch roles from behind the camera to filling his freezer. Together, they embark on a classic Alaskan adventure: fishing for rockfish, setting shrimp pots, and hunting Sitka blacktail deer, all while reminiscing about past trips and discovering new ways the wilderness shapes them.

programme.title: MeatEater - Wyoming Sage Grouse
programme.desc: Steve and his friend Ronny Boehme head to the vast sagebrush seas of Southeast Wyoming to hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater - Prince of Wales: Sitka Blacktail Deer Part 1
programme.desc: Late October in Southeast Alaska finds Steve, Ryan Callahan, and Mark Borman chasing Sitka blacktail deer. As the bucks begin their descent from the alpine into dense timber, the trio faces the challenge of hunting in the thick, damp rainforest. While navigating through muskeg patches and testing their luck with calling techniques, they must contend with unpredictable weather and tough terrain. When deer prove elusive, they turn to fishing and foraging.

programme.title: MeatEater - Mud and Trees
programme.desc: Wildlife biologist Parker Hall hosts Steve in Missouri for a weekend of chasing giant flathead catfish and gray squirrels along the Missouri River.

programme.title: MeatEater - Blue Mountain Bugles
programme.desc: Steve and Janis Putelis head into the wilds of eastern Washington’s Blue Range for an action-packed archery elk hunt. Janis calls multiple bulls into close range, and Steve connects on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.

programme.title: MeatEater - Ghosts of the Chesapeake Part 1
programme.desc: Steve and his friends Ryan Callaghan and Steve Kendrot join up for a Chesapeake Bay adventure as they pursue blue crabs and sika deer.

programme.title: MeatEater - Ghosts of the Chesapeake Part 2
programme.desc: The adventure in Maryland continues as the guys have a successful sika deer hunt and throw an East Shore crab boil with local oysters and a homemade smoked sika deer ham.

programme.title: MeatEater - Turkey Troubles
programme.desc: Steve and Janis Putelis escort first-time hunters Tracy and Maggie on a Montana turkey hunt. Emotions run high as the new hunters deal with failures and successes.

programme.title: MeatEater - Borderland Bucks Part 1
programme.desc: Steve and friends Ryan Callaghan and Mark Kenyon head south of the border in search of Coues deer on an old Mexican ranch that feels lost to time.

programme.title: MeatEater - Borderland Bucks Part 2
programme.desc: An action-filled hunt in Mexico leads to a surplus of venison, so locals teach the guys how to produce wild game tamales from scratch.

programme.title: Pod Save America (LIVE(ish) from the Obama Presidential Center)
programme.desc: Jon, Lovett, Tommy, and Dan record from Chicago after the dedication ceremony of the Obama Presidential Center, reacting to Barack and Michelle Obama's speeches, what it felt like to walk through the museum for the first time, and what lessons Democrats in the Trump era can take from Obama. Then, they react to the emerging details of Donald Trump's MOU with Iran, which he signed at the Palace of Versailles, JD Vance's efforts to seem like a normal person during his book tour, and why the algae in the Reflecting Pool is such an apt metaphor for Trump's presidency.

programme.title: What a Day (What If Abortion Meant Prison... Or Worse?)
programme.desc:
The 2022 Dobbs decision that reversed Roe v. Wade promised to return the issue of abortion back to the states. But for anti-abortion activists, that’s never been good enough. Some anti-abortion activists are pushing bills that could see women who get abortions imprisoned. And with the number of people who have gotten abortions only increasing over the last year, that’s a lot of punishment they want to mete out. Jessica Valenti, founder of the Substack Abortion, Every Day, has been tracking abortion rights for more than a decade. We talked about why the anti-abortion movement has gotten more extreme – and what we can do about it.
And in headlines, Republicans blast Trump’s new Iran memorandum, the Supreme Court rules marijuana users can own guns, and you heard it here first: the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is still green.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (Shady JD (Vance))
programme.desc: Is there anything that Vice President JD Vance hasn’t messed up? From political messaging, to lecturing the Pope about theology, Vance’s public appearances are often cringe inducing. But this week, Alex digs into the pernicious actions he’s taking behind the scenes. First she’s joined by California Attorney General Rob Bonta who reveals how much damage Vance’s anti-fraud task force is doing to legitimate businesses, and impacting those who need their services like hospice care. Then Alex speaks to Majority Report host Sam Seder about what it means to have a dishonest and bad actor like Vance waiting in the wings to be the next President.

programme.title: Hysteria (What in the World (Cup))
programme.desc: Erin and Alyssa round up the latest news from JD Vance’s embarrassing book tour, Bari Weiss’s next fail-upwards, and the future of the USPS’s independence. Then Megan Gailey (Sports Bitches) joins to talk sports news from the UFC to the Knicks and the rise of heteropessimism. They wrap up with a petty conversation about nun podcasts and takeout orders.

programme.title: What a Day (What If Abortion Meant Prison... Or Worse?)
programme.desc:
The 2022 Dobbs decision that reversed Roe v. Wade promised to return the issue of abortion back to the states. But for anti-abortion activists, that’s never been good enough. Some anti-abortion activists are pushing bills that could see women who get abortions imprisoned. And with the number of people who have gotten abortions only increasing over the last year, that’s a lot of punishment they want to mete out. Jessica Valenti, founder of the Substack Abortion, Every Day, has been tracking abortion rights for more than a decade. We talked about why the anti-abortion movement has gotten more extreme – and what we can do about it.
And in headlines, Republicans blast Trump’s new Iran memorandum, the Supreme Court rules marijuana users can own guns, and you heard it here first: the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is still green.

programme.title: Strict Scrutiny (SCOTUS Deals Another Blow to Multiracial Democracy)
programme.desc: Kate and Leah are joined by Democracy Docket’s Marc Elias to break down the Supreme Court’s shameful assault on multiracial democracy with its ruling on the Voting Rights Act case, Louisiana v. Callais.

programme.title: What a Day (Trump’s Senioritis)
programme.desc:
On today’s show, we are diving into the tough week our commander in chief just had. From MMA fights no one seems very excited about… to the potential end of warrantless surveillance because of an unpopular Trump hire, our dear president has been taking some big losses. Oh, and not to mention, the war with Iran is still going on. But Trump doesn’t seem to care about any of it! So to talk more about Donald Trump’s apparent case of senioritis, we called up Tim Miller, host of The Bulwark Podcast.
And in headlines: Elon Musk becomes the world’s first trillionaire, a poll finds Trump is losing favor with Independents, and a federal judge agrees to extend a court-ordered block on Trump’s $1.8 billion slush fund.

programme.title: Offline with Jon Favreau (Has The Left Finally Figured Out the Internet?)
programme.desc: Ever heard a popular TikToker wax poetic about who they’re voting for? They might be getting paid…and not disclosing by whom. Kyle Tharp, author of the Chaotic Era newsletter, joins Offline to discuss the emergence of pay-for-play political influencers and whether Democrats have made any progress in figuring out the internet. He and Jon talk about the decline of Ben Shapiro and the Daily Wire, the new success of legacy media on TikTok, and the intense grassroots anger around data centers.

programme.title: Stuntwoman Angels
programme.desc: Motion picture executive Jonathan Tobias hires Bosley and the Angels to track down someone dressed as one of Robin Hood's Merry Men who has been shooting arrows into studio personnel. Tobias knows that the arrows are aimed at him when his chauffeur is hit. Charlie arranges for the Angels to enroll in stunt school and then go to work as stuntwomen on a picture called "Marian and her Merry Maids" -- a bad remake of an old Errol Flynn classic. The Angels become friendly with one of the maids, Ellen Travers, and her husband, Jeff. Ellen, a former rodeo queen, has become enamored with movies. Jeff is trying desperately to get her to quit the movies and go back home with him. Bosley learns that one of the security guards, Jake Webner, was in the original picture with his idol -- Errol Flynn. Ellen Travers is hit with an arrow while driving a wagon during one of the scenes. It is Jake who has been dressing as Robin Hood's man with the bow and arrow, trying to get to Tobias because he feels that Tobias has been making bad movies. Jake is eventually caught while shooting an arrow into the director's foot. However, Bosley discovers that Jake was on guard duty when Ellen Travers was shot. Her husband, Jeff, who was trying to scare her into giving up movies, really shot her. Jake and Jeff are both taken into custody.

programme.title: Attack Angels
programme.desc: Edward Tustin, head of a group trying to take over Western Techtronics, engages efficiency expert, Dr. John Reardon of Executive Assistants, to have the four members of Techtronics who oppose his takeover killed. Reardon has hypnotized three girls for the job; Darlene Warren, Cynthia Weaver and Francine Miller. He uses motivational Pavlovian and Sensory conditioning and the subjects kill without fear, passion or memory. After the Chairman of the Board and a Vice President of Techtronics are killed and Francine Miller's body is found with them, the Angels are hired by company President, Steve Briggs. Briggs realizes he is next on the list. Kelly and Kris join Techtronics as executive trainees and Julie applies as Executive Assistant. She is hypnotized by Dr. Reardon, who uses flashing strobe effects, a candle and a water tank. After Julie has become completely programmed, she is sent to kill Briggs and almost succeeds when the suspicious Kelly and Kris arrive and save him. Julie is then deprogrammed by a doctor who explains that fire and water are two of the strongest hypnotic symbols. Reardon, Tustin and their associates are taken into custody.

programme.title: Angel On A Roll
programme.desc: After twelve different banks have been robbed of a quarter of a million dollars in hundred dollar bills, the Angels are called in to investigate. All the banks have the same type of automatic teller. Ted Markham, a computer and electronics genius who designed and installed the automatic tellers, has quit his job. Kris volunteers to become acquainted with Markham with the intention of obtaining some of the bills so that the serial numbers may be traced. Kris follows Markham to a poker parlor where he takes out a stack of $100 bills and with the help of a wrist calculator is a big winner. Ted and Kris are attracted to each other and they take off for Las Vegas in Kris' car. While in Las Vegas, Kris manages to get some of the bills away from Ted. She phones the serial numbers to Bosley and soon learns that they are part of the missing money. After Ted has won $300,000, he is asked to leave Las Vegas by the casino owner. On the way out of town, Ted stops at a bank while Kris waits in the car. When they arrive back in California, at the spot where Ted left his car, Kelly, Julie and Bosley are waiting with the police to take Ted into custody. Bosley then finds that Ted is free to go due to the fact that when he deposited the money in the bank in Las Vegas, he had paid back each of the twelve banks with interest, and they will not prosecute. Even though Ted and Kris have strong feelings for each other, they realize their relationship would not be the same -- so they sadly part.

programme.title: Mr. Galaxy
programme.desc: Ron Gates, a young body builder, has several accidents while training with his manager, Artie Weaver, for the Mr. Galaxy title. The Angels are called in to find out who is trying to injure Ron. One of the suspects is the current Mr. Galaxy, Chuck Wilde. Another is Wilde's manager, Norman Powers. Another suspect is Danny Barr, owner of the arena with heavy underworld connections. At one time Ron Gates was a boxer who had a management contract with Barr, but who, after almost killing another boxer, quit boxing and took up body building. Barr has always been angry with Ron for walking out on him. Kris stays close to Ron to keep her eye on him. Kelly poses as a staff reporter for a leading body building magazine. Julie and Bosley are customers at the gym called Sportworld. Selena Hansen, the wife of the boxer Ron Gates almost killed, is also a suspect. Ron keeps away from all confrontations, and when Kris questions why he doesn't defend himself, he says he will never hit anyone again. Ron ultimately wins the Mr. Galaxy title. After several more brushes with death, the Angels discover it is Danny Barr who has been trying to get rid of Ron and he is taken into custody.

programme.title: He Married An Angel
programme.desc: The Angels are hired by Barbara Stone and her sister, Jane, to track down John Thornwood. He has swindled them out of $100,000 by promising to marry Jane and persuading her to invest money in a phony South American oil exploration venture. The Angels track Thornwood to an apartment complex. Kris, posing as a confidence artist, rents the apartment next to his. Bosley poses as a Texan from whom she has swindled $50,000. Kris and Thornwood become friends, and he confesses to her that he has another scheme going. Kelly and Julie follow Thornwood to a museum owned by plain-looking Monica Thackery. Thornwood is posing as Professor Thornwood and has asked the smitten Monica to marry him and deposit $150,000 to an account supposedly for importing some pieces of art. Charlie, Bosley and the Angels find that Monica is the daughter of an eastern syndicate head, Joe Fenell. Thornwood is planning a phony marriage ceremony with Monica and then to withdraw the money and run. The Angels trick Thornwood into a real marriage with Monica because she is so much in love with him. They figure that her father will keep Thornwood in line. Thornwood, finding that he has been tricked, tries to escape, but is caught by Joe Fenell and his henchmen. Fenell warns him to be good to Monica or else. Thornwood knows that he has to stick with Monica and be a good husband...or else.

programme.title: Taxi Angels
programme.desc: The Angels are called in to investigate, when Tom Archer, who owns a cab company with his wife Laurie, is badly burned as his cab explodes. Laurie Archer reveals that they have been getting threatening phone calls and someone obviously is trying to put them out of business. Kris takes a job as a waitress across the street from the cab garage. Julie becomes a driver and Kelly, a dispatcher. Two employees, Jake Barnett, ex-con, and Sarge, a shell-shocked hallucinating casualty of World War II, are both under suspicion. It seems Tom Archer had fired Sarge and advised him to go to the V.A. hospital for treatment. Later, Julie's cab catches fire and she and her passenger escape just in time. Jake advises Julie that Sarge's ex-wife was killed by a cab and he is starting the fires to put Tom and Laurie's cab company out of business. The Angels go to Sarge's room, but he escapes through a window, goes up on the roof, and tries to jump to the next building. He misses and is killed. The Angels then find out that Sarge's wife was not killed by a cab. Instead, the woman who Jake lived with was the victim of the accident. Julie takes Jake out in her cab with a tape recorder on -- the others listening -- and gets Jake to admit that he is really the guilty one. He takes off in the cab -- Kris arrives and she and Julie pursue him and trap him at the Los Angeles River. The other Angels praise Julie for her driving expertise and she admits she learned it from an old boyfriend in her neighborhood who drove stolen cars.

programme.title: Angel On The Line
programme.desc: Mary and her very attractive friend, Lois, go to the Hot Line Club, a singles club with a phone at each table. The phone rings and the caller asks to speak to Lois. He then tells her that she is so pretty he has to kill her. Lois leaves the club. She is followed, and in an effort to escape, runs in front of a car, is hit and killed. Mary calls in the Angels to investigate. They all go to the Hot Line Club. The entertainment is hypnotist, Margo, who gets Kelly into the act. Kelly later gives Margo her business card. Afterwards, Kelly sits with Mary. Soon the phone rings and Mary answers. The voice asks to speak with Kelly. When she gets on, he tells her that she is so pretty he has to kill her. Mary recognizes it as the same voice that called Lois. When Kelly leaves the club, she sees a car following her and writes down the license number. Later she is contacted by a man named Edward Ford who says he is a writer. He tells Kelly that Margo gave him her name and number. They arrange to meet for lunch. Kelly is suspicious of him and contacts the other Angels who find out that his car is registered in the name of Paul Factor. Kelly leaves the luncheon and goes back to the Hot Line Club in an effort to get information about Ford from Margo. When Margo arrives, she takes Kelly out to a back alley, pulls a knife and tries to stab her. The Angels and Bosley arrive in time to rescue her. During the struggle, Margo's wig and false eyelashes come off, revealing "her" to be Edward Ford who really is Paul Factor.

programme.title: Chorus Line Angels
programme.desc: The Angels are hired by Ruth Traina, who has invested $150,000 in a Darian Mason Production, to investigate the disappearance of two of the best dancers. Kelly auditions a job as a dancer; Julie is her agent/manager and Kris poses as a writer/critic doing a story on Mason. Another of the better dancers, Nancy, disappears after being caught going through Mason's files. Mason then catches Kris going through his files and threatens her. The Angels and Bosley find that Mason has deliberately hired theinexperienced and gotten rid of the good ones to make the audition a failure, since he can't possibly pay off all of his investors. Kelly and Julie come to Kris' rescue. They then find and free the three missing dancers. The cast, including Kelly, decide to go through the audition since they feel that they owe it to themselves to at least try. The Las Vegas executives they audition for express great interest in the dancers.

programme.title: Stuntwoman Angels
programme.desc: Motion picture executive Jonathan Tobias hires Bosley and the Angels to track down someone dressed as one of Robin Hood's Merry Men who has been shooting arrows into studio personnel. Tobias knows that the arrows are aimed at him when his chauffeur is hit. Charlie arranges for the Angels to enroll in stunt school and then go to work as stuntwomen on a picture called "Marian and her Merry Maids" -- a bad remake of an old Errol Flynn classic. The Angels become friendly with one of the maids, Ellen Travers, and her husband, Jeff. Ellen, a former rodeo queen, has become enamored with movies. Jeff is trying desperately to get her to quit the movies and go back home with him. Bosley learns that one of the security guards, Jake Webner, was in the original picture with his idol -- Errol Flynn. Ellen Travers is hit with an arrow while driving a wagon during one of the scenes. It is Jake who has been dressing as Robin Hood's man with the bow and arrow, trying to get to Tobias because he feels that Tobias has been making bad movies. Jake is eventually caught while shooting an arrow into the director's foot. However, Bosley discovers that Jake was on guard duty when Ellen Travers was shot. Her husband, Jeff, who was trying to scare her into giving up movies, really shot her. Jake and Jeff are both taken into custody.

programme.title: Phony Express

programme.title: Hula-La-La
programme.desc: Dance-teacher Stooges learn to hula.

programme.title: Baby Sitters' Jitters
programme.desc: Estranged wife hires boys to baby-sit.

programme.title: Booty and the Beast
programme.desc: Boys are locked in with a lion.

programme.title: We Want Our Mummy
programme.desc: The boys seek a lost Egyptologist.

programme.title: The Rutabaga Story
programme.desc: There's a problem in Hooterville: no farmer seems able to grow a cash crop on account of the quality of the soil. But as it turns out from Oliver's (Eddie Albert) soil and mineral research, the oft misunderstood rutabaga is an ideal choice.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 1)
programme.desc: Green Acres goes Hollywood in this first installment of a two-part season finale. When Arnold the pig appears in a play at the Hooterville Theater, Lisa (Eva Gabor) arranges for an old friend to give Arnold a chance on the big screen.

programme.title: A Star Named Arnold Is Born (Part 2)
programme.desc: It's a swine-sational third season finale for Green Acres, as Oliver and Lisa chaperone Arnold, the Ziffel's talented pig, to Hollywood for his big screen test. There's only one problem, Lisa failed to mention that Arnold is not your normal actor.

programme.title: Guess Who's Not Going To The Luau
programme.desc: Hooterville is abuzz as the townsfolk enter a contest sponsored by Peterson's Poi Juice, with a trip to Hawaii as the top prize. But no one expects the winner to be Arnold, the Ziffels' talented pig. Maybe a pig might fly after all, to the islands!

programme.title: The Rummage Sale
programme.desc: A charity rummage sale asks for donated clothing. Being among the wealthier residents of Hooterville, the organizers look to the Douglases. It isn't long before Oliver has to stop Lisa from giving away his entire wardrobe!

programme.title: Eb's Romance
programme.desc: Love is in the air when Eb is quickly engaged to Loreli Appleby, whom he has only known for a day. Eb tries to impress Loreli's father, a farmer, but in typical fashion, he accidentally ruins his future father-in-law's cabbage crop.

programme.title: Hail To The Fire Chief
programme.desc: When Fire Chief Joe Carson is looking for a way to go to Miami, he hires Oliver as his assistant, hoping Oliver will pay for the trip. The plan backfires, no pun intended, when Oliver starts pointing out what a poor job the chief has been doing.

programme.title: The Candidate
programme.desc: The highways in Hooterville are in dire need of repair. Oliver is fed up and believes he can make a difference by complaining to his state representative, Ben Hanks. But Hanks' popularity proves greater than his sense of responsibility.

programme.title: Handy Lessons
programme.desc: Lisa feels like she should be contributing more and wants to start working in Oliver's construction business. Lisa is like a fish out of water as she arrives to the Monroes' workshop in overalls and causes one problem after another.

programme.title: A Husband For Eleanor
programme.desc: Lisa plays bovine matchmaker when Oliver and Lisa decide it is time for their female cow, Eleanor, to start providing milk for the farm. Oliver makes arrangements for Eleanor to mooooove in with the neighbor's bull, but Lisa has other ideas.

programme.title: Old Mail Day
programme.desc: The townsfolk look forward to "Old Mail Day", when once a year undelivered mail is handed out. The delayed mail includes a letter all the way from 1917! Oliver is upset by the poor service, but his complaints land with more impact than he intended.

programme.title: The Agricultural Student
programme.desc: When word spreads that an agricultural student is coming to Hooterville to study with county agent Hank Kimball, no one seems to be interested. However, things change when the folks discover that the student is an attractive blonde.

programme.title: How Hooterville Was Floundered
programme.desc: This episode tells the unusual tale of Hooterville's founding. As the town prepares for its centennial, Oliver announces a reenactment of the town's origins and casts himself and Lisa in the roles of Horace and Doris Hooter.

programme.title: The Blue Feather
programme.desc: When a blue feather arrives in the mail for Lisa, her Hungarian roots tell her that trouble is brewing. The feather is a symbol of malfortune to come, and she suspects it was sent by Hungarian gypsies, a warning that they are out to get her.

programme.title: Everywhere A Chick Chick
programme.desc: Don't count chickens before they hatch! Oliver thinks he's laid a gold egg of an idea when he decides to dive beak first into the business of raising chickens. Lisa, on the other hand, sees little difference between raising chickens and children.

programme.title: A Prize In Each And Every Package
programme.desc: All that glitters is not frosted cereal. After a jewelry heist in Chicago, the robbers foolishly stash their booty in the grain bin of a cereal manufacturer. Oliver and Lisa discover the jewels in their bowl of Crickly Wickly!

programme.title: Law Partners
programme.desc: Oliver Wendell Douglas returns from farm to firm... law firm, that is. When a young and ambitious local graduates from law school, he looks for a potential partner in Hooterville. Oliver is the logical choice. Lisa and Eb help the new venture.

programme.title: My Son the Lover
programme.desc: Popular girl steals J.J.'s heart.

programme.desc: Florida is an angry whirlwind.

programme.desc: Bob paints detailed trees in winter.

programme.desc: Bob's landscape fades into infinity.

programme.desc: Brilliant foliage surrounds a quiet pond.

programme.title: Autumn Exhibition
programme.desc: Bob paints a peaceful autumn path.

programme.title: Majestic Peaks
programme.desc: Bob paints snow-covered mountains.

programme.title: Golden Morning Mist
programme.desc: Branches bend over a misty stream.

programme.title: Portrait Of Gomez
programme.desc: Morticia's pet plant Cleopatra gobbles up her favorite photo of Gomez from the family album. When the original photographer is found and asked to make a duplicate picture, it is discovered that he is now with the Department of Motor Vehicles.

programme.title: Morticia's Dilemma
programme.desc: Gomez plays host to Don Xavier Molines, a family friend during his childhood in Spain, and his daughter, Consuella. Morticia's hospitality is put to the test when Consuella reveals that she has been promised in marriage to Gomez since childhood.

programme.title: Christmas With The Addams Family
programme.desc: When Wednesday's and Pugsley's faith in Santa Claus is shaken, Uncle Fester is chosen to back up Santa in case the real St. Nick is too busy to come. However, Fester-Santa gets stuck in the chimney, so each of the other adults fill in for him.

programme.title: Uncle Fester, Tycoon
programme.desc: Fester is so smitten upon receipt of a photo of his love, a bearded carnival lady, that he borrows stamp money to mail her a proposal. Morticia dons a beard and poses as the mother to convince Fester that he will have to work to support a wife.

programme.title: Morticia & Gomez Vs. Fester & Grandmama
programme.desc: Because she thinks Fester (Jackie Coogan) and Grandmama (Blossom Rock) are spoiling the children, Morticia (Carolyn Jones) hires a governess. Insulted, Fester paints a white line down the center of the Addams house, dividing it in half.

programme.title: Fester Goes On A Diet
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez see Fester conferring with TV muscleman Jack LaGrann (Jack LaLanne), and think that Fester is planning a television career. However, Uncle Fester confides to Lurch that he has to go on a secret diet and exercise.

programme.title: The Great Treasure Hunt
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez find Grandfather Pegleg's treasure map and summon Captain Grimby. They hope to charter his boat for the treasure hunt. But after meeting the Captain, Morticia is careful to keep the nature of the mission secret.

programme.title: Ophelia Finds Romance
programme.desc: Ophelia Frump is madly in love with Horatio Bartholomew, the man who has everything, except the truth about her relatives. Morticia, not convinced by Bartholomew, persuades playboy Cousin Itt to stir the embers of his past romance with Ophelia.

programme.title: Pugsley's Allowance
programme.desc: Pugsley shocks his parents when he gives up his new atomic reactor as kid stuff and announces that he wants to find his own job. His first try is as a medical assistant to Dr. Bird. He turns up, scalpel in hand, ready to help during an operation.

programme.title: Happy Birthday, Grandma Frump
programme.desc: Grandma Frump (guest star Margaret Hamilton) comes to the mistaken conclusion that her daughter, Morticia (Carolyn Jones), and Gomez (John Astin) plan to pack her off to an old folks home. Actually, they plan to send her to a beauty farm.

programme.title: Morticia, The Decorator
programme.desc: Morticia, in order to be part of the National Beautification Program, decides to become a home decorator and offers her services to the neighbors. Next door neighbor, and insurance agent, Joe Digby hires Morticia in hopes of selling them insurance.

programme.title: Ophelia Visits Morticia
programme.desc: Ophelia taunts Uncle Fester into announcing that he is going to join the Peace Corps. Fester feels he's a cinch to be accepted because Gomez plans to pull strings in Washington on his behalf. The entire family agrees to help him study for the exam.

programme.title: Addams Cum Laude
programme.desc: Allyn Joslyn guest-stars as Sam Hilliard, a former truant officer who now heads a private school where Wednesday and Pugsley are enrolled. When Hilliard breaks under the strain of dealing with the Addams children, Gomez buys the school.

programme.title: Cat Addams
programme.desc: Dr. Marvin P. Gunderson is summoned to examine Kitty Kat, the ailing lion pet of the Addams Family. Morticia decides that it is the doctor who needs help. To build his confidence, human members of the family pretend to be ill so he can affect cures.

programme.title: Lurch's Little Helper
programme.desc: Morticia's concern that Lurch is overworked leads Gomez to the operating table to assemble a custom-built secondary butler. Lurch lets the robot do all the work and then begins to feel that he isn't needed anymore.

programme.title: The Addams Policy
programme.desc: When Uncle Fester incinerates the family's stuffed bear with his flamethrower, Morticia reports the accident to the insurance company. Mr. Henson, the head of the company, sends his assistant, Mr. Digby, to handle the case.

programme.title: Lurch's Grand Romance
programme.desc: Lurch suffers from unrequited love over Tiny Trivia (guest star Diane Jergens), Morticia's visiting school chum who only has eyes for show business. The entire family decides to try to turn their stolid butler in to a new personality.

programme.title: Lunatic
programme.desc: With Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) on the run from Sheriff Stilinski (Linden Ashby), Stiles (Dylan O'Brien) must help Scott (Tyler Posey) face his second full moon. Meanwhile, Jackson (Colton Haynes) tries to get closer to Allison (Crystal Reed).

programme.title: Wolf's Bane
programme.desc: Clues, including a prior encounter with Derek (Tyler Hoechlin), bring Jackson (Colton Haynes) to a dangerous revelation about Scott (Tyler Posey)…and his actions begin to threaten not only Scott's life, but his own as well.

programme.title: Co-Captain
programme.desc: With startling revelations weighing in on him, Scott (Tyler Posey) finds himself scrambling to protect the people he loves, including Allison (Crystal Reed) who begins to follow clues left by her Aunt Kate about her family history.

programme.title: Formality
programme.desc: While struggling with family secrets, Allison (Crystal Reed) focuses on the Winter Formal dance. Meanwhile, as the tensions between werewolves and hunters rise to a boiling point, Scott (Tyler Posey) struggles to protect both friends and enemies.

programme.title: Code Breaker
programme.desc: In the season finale, Scott McCall (Tyler Posey) discovers one last clue which allows him to enlist both friends -- and even a previous enemy -- in order to stop the mysterious killings and win back Allison (Crystal Reed), the girl he loves.

programme.title: Omega
programme.desc: When Lydia (Holland Roden) goes missing from the hospital, werewolves and hunters alike begin a search to discover not only where she is, but what she may have become. Meanwhile Jackson's (Colton Haynes) body seems to be fighting against the bite.

programme.title: Shape Shifted
programme.desc: The night before a full moon, Isaac's father (John Wesley Shipp) is murdered. When Isaac (Daniel Sharman) is arrested, Scott (Tyler Posey), Stiles (Dylan O'Brien) and Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) resolve to break him out before the Argents get to him.

programme.title: Ice Pick
programme.desc: Scott (Tyler Posey) tries to stop Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) from creating new werewolves, while Chris Argent (JR Bourne) decides to officially begin Allison's (Crystal Reed) training. Meanwhile, the new creature in Beacon Hills takes another victim.

programme.title: Abomination
programme.desc: When the shapeshifter strikes again, Scott (Tyler Posey) has to recruit a reluctant Allison (Crystal Reed) to acquire a rare book from Gerard's (guest star Michael Hogan) collection, while also once again surviving dinner with the Argents.

programme.title: Venomous
programme.desc: Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) becomes convinced that Lydia (Holland Roden) is the shapeshifter they're after since she seemed immune to its toxin, forcing Scott (Tyler Posey) to gather his friends -- and even some enemies -- to protect her.

programme.title: Frenemy
programme.desc: With startling new information about the shapeshifter, Scott (Tyler Posey) and Stiles (Dylan O'Brien) hurry to protect their friends while Allison (Crystal Reed) is given an investigative task by her family. And there is "panic at the disco."

programme.desc: George Hahn breaks down the week’s biggest Prof G stories in business, tech, politics, and culture.

programme.title: EXPOSED: How Trump and POWERFUL ELITES Manipulate EVERYTHING (ft. Pablo Torre)
programme.desc: Pablo Torre on billionaire power, prediction market corruption, sports culture wars, and the Epstein class.

programme.title: Trump’s LOYALTY FUND Sparks OUTRAGE as MAGA FRACTURES in Record PRIMARY BLOODBATH
programme.desc: Scott & Tarlov on Trump's IRS slush fund, Massie's primary, AI misinformation, and a San Diego hate crime.

programme.title: Aswath Damodaran Says "There’s No Place to Hide in Stocks"
programme.desc: NYU's Damodaran on market concerns, the top 2026 stock pick, and advice for financially anxious young people.

programme.title: A Founder’s Mission to Solve Hunger in America
programme.desc: Sharebite’s Dilip Rao shares his mission to fight hunger and build a purpose-driven company.

programme.title: Uncertain About Your Relationship? You Need This…
programme.desc: How do you know if you’re with the right person — or if you're just telling yourself you are? If you’re questioning the relationship more than enjoying it, Matthew Hussey might have the clarity you need. So what does finding your person actually look like, and what signs should you pay attention to? Expect to learn how to know when it’s time to leave a relationship, the 5 questions to ask yourself if you’re unsure about your relationship, why people struggle so hard with letting others go, how women can best support their man and visa versa, how important attractiveness is to both men and women in a successful relationship, ways to learnt to be more vulnerable and much more…

programme.title: The Case Against Condoms & Fake Friendship
programme.desc: Why do humans act so complicated? We’re simple creatures, farts are funny, everyone does awkward stuff, yet we all pretend we don’t. Maybe life gets better when you lean into the weird instead of hiding it. So how do you embrace being different and find the fun instead of stressing about it? Expect to learn how Rick’s brain works, what his favourite show and jokes are and how they shaped him, the top things that are difficult to look cool while doing, why using "condoms" with friends are useless, how to handle our people pleasing tendencies for good, why humans are so hard to interact with at times, the awkward feelings of becoming intimate with someone, the biggest message Rick Glassman wants to give out in the world and much more…

programme.title: The Most Important Daily Habits For Health & Longevity
programme.desc: Determining the best actions to take for enhancing our health and extending our lives has grown more challenging. There are an unlimited number of wellness approaches at our disposal, thankfully Dr Patrick has dedicated her professional life to identifying the most evidence-based strategies for improving our health and longevity. Expect to learn if low omega 3s are worse for you than smoking cigarettes, what Rhonda's thoughts are on vaping and nicotine, the best foods to boost your metabolism, the once a week workout that can de-age your heart by 20 years, how to get the benefits of heat exposure if you don't have a sauna, the terrifying health risks of being too sedentary and much more...

programme.title: Why Fear & Horror Feels Kinda Good
programme.desc: Why are we drawn to fear? This fascination often balances us the edge of life and death, infused with a dash of excitement. From his beginnings as a Navy SEAL to becoming a renowned YouTuber, Mr. Ballen possesses a unique storytelling talent. He captivates us with some of the most spine-chilling true stories, drawn both from his experiences and the lives of others. Expect to learn why so many people are obsessed with True Crime and scary stories, the 3 terrifying but true stories MrBallen has prepared in this episode today, what being a NAVY Seal taught MrBallen about life & the backlash he has gotten from the NAVY Seal community by sharing his story, what it means to live a good life, if there is life after death, how to reinvent your entire life, what it takes to tell a good story and much more...

programme.title: The Man Who Was Never Born
programme.desc: Martin Landau stars as the time-traveler Andro who is desperately trying to stop the birth of a crazed 20th century inventor whose bacterium invention turns humans into hideous mutants. Shirley Knight co-stars in this classic, touching episode.

programme.title: O.B.I.T.
programme.desc: Both a cautionary tale and a tight courtroom drama, this haunting episode explores the fear and hostility that result when all privacy is eliminated...and all secrets are revealed! Jeff Corey, Peter Breck and Alan Baxter star.

programme.title: The Human Factor
programme.desc: In an isolated, snowbound outpost where a handful of people are on alert against enemy attack, a crazed Army major (Harry Guardino) is haunted by the ghost of a soldier he left to die. Sally Kellerman co-stars in one of her earliest TV appearances.

programme.title: Corpus Earthling
programme.desc: Two rock samples in Dr. Temple's (Barry Atwater) lab conceal protoplasmic alien invaders capable of controlling a human "corpus." Enter Paul Cameron (Robert Culp)...who can telepathically hear the "rocks" plotting their take-over of the world.

programme.title: Nightmare
programme.desc: A six-man strike force is captured on the planet Ebon and brutally interrogated. And when they are suddenly allowed the "respect due a conquered enemy," the prisoners to conclude that there is a traitor among them...someone who must be killed!

programme.title: It Crawled Out Of The Woodwork
programme.desc: Writer-producer Joseph Stefano creates both a macabre parable of the destructive potential of nuclear energy and a pulsating tale of terror featuring a superbly understated performance by Ed Asner. Scott Marlow and Michael Forrest also star.

programme.title: The Borderland
programme.desc: In this highly charged, fascinating tale that explores the concept of alternate dimensions, a wealthy industrialist (Barry Jones) attempts to contact his dead son by funding a scientist's experiments in prying open the door to "the borderland."

programme.title: The Zanti Misfits
programme.desc: They creep. They crawl. They kill. When the rulers of the planet Zanti are allowed to exile their criminals to our world, the screeching misfits will demonstrate to the military just what the word "invasion" really means. Bruce Dern stars.

programme.title: Controlled Experiment
programme.desc: Befuddled by the strange local custom of murder, two Martian administrators (Barry Morse and Carroll O'Connor) visit Earth to study one such crime and find themselves tempted to tamper with events in this whimsical tale from "The Outer Limits."

programme.title: Amor perdido
programme.desc: A beautiful young girl named Amalia.(played by actress Amalia Aguilar). with a talent for Rumba, wants to become a glamorous cabaret singer. Her dream is encouraged by her best friend, Ernesto, (Tito Novaro), who also happens to be a gifted composer. While working towards her career goal, Amalia meets the ultimate "bad-boy", a dashing gangster named Luis, (Victor Junco). She is quickly swept off her feet and falls in love with him. Unfortunately, Luis has a jealous streak and a rather possessive personality, especially when it comes to women. He also has a prior girlfriend named Celia, (Yadira Jimenez). Celia also happens to share similar traits with Luis and is filled with jealous rage toward Amelia. So much so that she causes her rival in love to have an accident which leaves Amalia's face horribly scarred-so much so that she must now wear a mask in public. Filled with shame and fear of rejection, she decides to leave Luis without explanation or saying farewell, Her actions anger her former lover, who thinks she left him for better career opportunities. So, he decides to kill poor disfigured Amalia.
